description: Persistent issue tracker. Decider appends new issues from Curmudgeon reviews, marks resolved when wins.json no longer contains the flagged text.

issues.description: VERDICT CATEGORY OVERLOAD: 'Misleading' verdict conflates 4 distinct failure modes: (1) data misrepresentation, (2) duplication/redundancy, (3) non-discrimination (observation explained by multiple models equally), (4) circular derivation. 23 WINs under one label implies systematic dishonesty, but many are simply non-discriminating observations. Consider subcategory 'Non-Discriminating' or move non-discriminating WINs to 'Std Model Explains'. Affects verdict taxonomy coherence and reviewer's interpretation of dome author intent.
issues.rationale_for_deferral: Verdict taxonomy restructuring requires human editorial decision on subcategory definitions

issues.description: GitHub PAT stored on disk in workspace FUSE mount at .workspace-sync-token (gitignored, chmod 600). Required so the dome-workspace-sync agent — which runs in its own ephemeral session at a different path — can authenticate pushes without depending on dome-review-clean existing in its session. File is local-only (never committed, verified via git check-ignore and git ls-files), so threat surface is limited to processes running as the workspace user on this machine. Cleanup options to consider when convenient: (a) replace static PAT with a short-lived GitHub App installation token minted on demand, (b) move secret to an OS keychain / credential helper that the agent can call into instead of a flat file, (c) at minimum, rotate the PAT periodically and document the rotation procedure (update .workspace-sync-token AND dome-review-clean/.git/config remote URL, then revoke old PAT). Not urgent — file is not exposed beyond the local VM workspace.
issues.rationale_for_deferral: Secure credential storage requires human decision on approach (GitHub App vs keychain vs rotation policy)

issues.description: The analyst pipeline does not currently form views on the dome's 10 Prospective Wins (PROS-001..PROS-008 plus WIN-057, WIN-058 which sit in both buckets). Mode 0 (new WIN onboarding) only triggers when the dome's confirmed WIN count exceeds ours, so prospective predictions are invisible to the review pipeline. CONTEXT: The PROS bucket is where the dome stages predictions before promoting them to confirmed wins. Tracking them would let us (a) pre-empt his promotion narrative — when he announces "PROS-001 confirmed, now WIN-070", we already have a critique ready; (b) catch silent suspensions at the prospective level (PROS-003 went SUSPENDED in the 2026-03-15 batch alongside WIN-010/WIN-025 and we missed it until today); (c) score his prospective hit rate over time as a separate accuracy figure that is actually predictive rather than retrodictive. PROPOSAL: Add a new analyst Mode 5 (prospective tracking) and a new data file data/prospective.json with one entry per PROS item. Each entry would capture: prediction text, stated test conditions, our pre-emptive critique, current dome status (active / promoted / suspended / removed), and link to the WIN entry if/when promoted. The poller already fetches the prospective section, so the data is available; just needs a Mode 5 to act on it. The new headline structure on the wins tab could grow a fifth metric for prospective hit rate once Mode 5 has produced enough data. DESIGN DECISIONS for analyst: should prospective items live in a separate file or as a section of wins.json? Should there be a new tab on the review site, or just additional rows in the WINs tab? Should the silent-failures counter pull in suspended-prospective items (currently FAIL-008 is the only one, manually backfilled today)?
issues.source: human discussion 2026-04-09 — surfaced during silent-failures counter design
issues.rationale_for_deferral: New analyst mode design and data file structure decision — needs analyst input rather than ad-hoc human implementation.

issues.description: Workspace-only analyst files not committed to git (partially resolved 2026-04-10: EXP-059, ISS-672-research now in git). Remaining workspace-only files are supplemental working notes (analysis summaries, research memos, cascade analysis) — lower risk than pipeline EXP outputs.
issues.notes: [2026-04-09] workspace-sync agent should commit these files. Decider cannot commit workspace-only files directly (FUSE filesystem ownership rules). [2026-04-10] Decider rescued EXP-059.json and ISS-672-research.json (critical expansion files). Workspace-sync agent should handle remaining files on next run.

issues.description: CAT-005 proposal: Analyst proposes new section "V51.1 Refraction Cascade: How One Parameter Fix Breaks Everything" covering 12+ affected WINs. Cascade failure analysis shows refraction needed is 13-27 degrees vs 0.03 degrees real. Human must decide: approve as new section (Part 2 or Section 7 extension), reject, or integrate as individual WIN patches.
issues.human_decision: Approved as new section under The Model tab (option a). New code_analysis sub-category tag requested for cascade-affected WINs. Analyst to write recommended proposal package — route to curmudgeon before fleshing out prose. See CAT-005.json human_decision block.
issues.gate_returned_reason: Curmudgeon gate (2026-04-10): ACCEPT_WITH_MODIFICATIONS — 7 blockers. Analyst must produce EXP-050-numerics.json, re-audit WIN flip table (per-WIN re-read required), withdraw cascade_dependent tag, use structural title, renumber to Section 1.8. Resubmit for second gate pass.

issues.description: External report #1 (Devilwench): Positive feedback only. No error claim, correction, or scientific content. "You're awesome don't ever change." No changes needed.
issues.fix_details: Not a problem report — positive feedback only. Analyst logged as reader engagement data point.

issues.description: PREDICTIONS CATALOG: Analyst Mode 0 priority — catalog all ~50 new prospective predictions (registered 2026-03-21 to 2026-04-04) into data/predictions.json. Each entry needs: id, claim, category, outcome, prospective (boolean), testability, derivation, test_window (with closes date), related_wins, registration_date, crypto_hash, restates_win (WIN ID or null), data_predates_registration (boolean), is_genuinely_prospective (boolean). CRITICAL: Many predictions recycle confirmed WINs with fresh SHA-256 timestamps — known: PRED-095=WIN-068, PRED-072=WIN-042, PRED-090 refs WIN-066, PRED-087 mirrors WIN-076, PRED-074 pre-reg data. Distinguish genuinely prospective from timestamped retrodictions.
issues.notes: [2026-04-10] Wave 1 classification complete (analyst proposal ISS-700-status): 86 entries in predictions.json now have entry_type, author_status, our_verdict. 36 newly classified: 2 genuinely prospective, 28 data-predates, 6 unfalsifiable model params. Waves 2 (15 genuinely prospective writeups) and 3 (11 recycled + ~40 backtested writeups + Part 6 prose) remain. Status kept assigned-analyst.

issues.description: UPDATE PART 6 PROSE: The dome author added SHA-256 crypto timestamps to new predictions — but analysis shows these are mostly recycled WINs with fresh timestamps, not genuinely prospective predictions. Section should: (1) note the SHA-256 methodology shift and its limitations (no independent verification mechanism), (2) explain that timestamps are real but predictions are largely post-hoc, (3) contrast with genuinely prospective predictions, (4) use {{PRED_*}} placeholders for counts (infrastructure ready). Cross-refs: EXP-053/054, data/predictions.json (populate first).
issues.notes: [2026-04-10] Part 6 prose update blocked on Wave 3 (analyst proposal ISS-702-status). Key finding already identified: author's "What Prospective Means" framework concedes backtested vs prospective distinction. 57/86 entries are author-labeled backtested. PRED-090 confirmed as post-hoc retrodiction. By author's own standard ~93% of 69 WINs are backtested. Prose will quote author's own framework once Wave 3 writeups complete. Status kept assigned-analyst.

issues.description: SHA-256 RECYCLING FORENSICS: The dome April 4 batch of ~28 "new prospective" predictions recycle already-confirmed WINs with fresh SHA-256 timestamps. Need: (1) curmudgeon forensic analysis of git commit timeline for April 4 batch — verify whether commits actually landed April 4 or were backdated; (2) per-prediction restates_win classification; (3) key argument: author responded to our timestamping critique by adding crypto envelopes around post-hoc content. Canary trap opentimestamps-provenance is probable_trigger.

EXP-054 OPENTIMESTAMPS GAP: The batch appended post-hoc sentences claim the dome's definition is "unverifiable" because monitor.py hardcodes targets. But 5 self-declared PROSPECTIVE WINs (035-039, weekly confirmations) claim the prediction was registered via OTS before data arrival. The post-hoc rebuttal must address the OTS claim directly rather than relying on monitor.py evidence that doesn't apply to these WINs. Related to Section 3.1b's existing OTS gap.
issues.notes: This is a subset of the wider OTS/SHA-256 investigation (ISS-703). The specific action needed: craft a different post-hoc rebuttal for OTS-claiming WINs that addresses the timestamp chain rather than the monitor.py hardcoding argument. Route to analyst as addendum to EXP-054 or new EXP. Blocked on ISS-703 SHA-256 forensics.

issues.description: EXP-060 PIPELINE: generate-html.js needs support for kill-shots.json data file to resolve {{KS_TOTAL}}, {{KS_STRAW_MAN_COUNT}}, {{KS_WAIT_AND_SEE_COUNT}} template placeholders in Part 5 HTML. data/kill-shots.json already created (6 entries). EXP-060 replacement_html uses these placeholders but they are currently unresolved. Current Part 5 uses EXP-057 prose (hardcoded). When pipeline supports it, swap EXP-057 HTML for EXP-060 HTML to get data-driven count injection.
issues.notes: Not urgent — existing Part 5 HTML is complete and correct via EXP-057. This is a refactoring to data-driven counts. Lower priority than content work.

issues.description: SEC-2b.7 W004/WIN-025 IDENTITY MAPPING: Section 2b.7 identifies W004 in results.json as the 2024 Eclipse 9-Station test (WIN-025), but dome raw-text/02-wins.txt defines W004 as "Noise Floor Limit (empirical recalibration at v50.2 established baseline -22.24 nT)" — a detection-threshold item, NOT the eclipse. The section's core "two scoreboards" argument (same observation simultaneously removed on wins tab and confirmed_strong in results.json) depends entirely on this W004=WIN-025 mapping being correct. Must re-fetch results.json live and dump W004 entry to verify. If mapping is wrong, section must be rewritten.
issues.notes: Curmudgeon Cycle 2 flagged as blocker — do not publish section 2b.7 until verified. Two hypotheses: (a) results.json uses different W-number namespace than wins page (W004 in results.json IS the eclipse despite wins-page W004 being noise-floor); (b) analyst misidentified the entry. Check results.json W004 entry title and prediction field (-10 ± 2 nT matches WIN-025 BOU baseline, not -22.24 nT noise-floor).

issues.description: SOCIAL: Keywords meta tag expansion for rebrand-resistance. Social analyst recommends expanding keywords meta tag to include ECM alternative names (Ovoid Cavity Model, Closed Toroidal Ovoid, dome cosmology) and numerical fingerprints (20015 km, 8537 km, firmament height) to survive potential model rename. See monitor/social/drafts/rebrand-resistance-strategy.md for full strategy.
issues.notes: Social draft action_needed: decide whether to expand keywords meta tag. Not deployed — needs human approval before committing to docs/.
